title: Does your company use, or plan to use, any AI system in business processes?
help: Include chatbots, scoring, recommendations, generative automation, computer vision or systems bought from vendors.

options.label: No, or exploration only with no deployment

title: Could the system fit a prohibited AI Act practice?
help: Examples: harmful subliminal manipulation, exploitation of vulnerabilities, social scoring, unauthorised remote biometric identification or emotion recognition in employment/education unless an exception applies.

title: Do you use AI for recruitment, CV screening, interviews or candidate ranking?
help: Include screening tools, role matching, automated assessment or interview prioritisation.

effects.value: Annex III: employment and worker management

title: Does AI support worker management, performance, shifts, promotion or dismissal decisions?
help: Assignment, evaluation or monitoring systems at work usually require high-risk analysis.

title: Does AI decide or influence credit, solvency, insurance pricing or financial eligibility?
help: Include scoring, approval/rejection, pricing, fraud or case prioritisation with meaningful impact on people.

effects.value: Annex III: access to essential private services

title: Is it used for admission, assessment, guidance or educational/professional certification?
help: Covers access to training, learning assessment, plagiarism detection with consequences and relevant career guidance.

effects.value: Annex III: education and vocational training

title: Does it affect access to essential public or private services?
help: For example benefits, healthcare, housing, emergency services, essential banking services or eligibility that matters to an individual.

title: Does it control or prioritise operations in critical infrastructure?
help: Think energy, transport, water, health, telecoms, safety or continuity of critical services.

title: Is AI a safety component of a regulated product or subject to conformity assessment?
help: Examples: machinery, medical devices, toys, lifts, radio equipment, automotive, aviation or products under Annex I.

effects.value: Art. 6.1 and Annex I: regulated product

title: Does it use biometrics, biometric categorisation or emotion recognition?
help: If the case appears in employment, education or remote identification, it needs a specific review and may be prohibited or high-risk.

title: Is it used in justice, migration, asylum, border control or public authority support?
help: Even if your company is a private provider, selling into or deploying in these contexts often activates high-risk analysis.

effects.value: Annex III: public authority, justice or migration

title: Is it a chatbot or assistant that interacts directly with people?
help: Include customer support, internal support, web agents or assistants that simulate human conversation.

title: Does it generate or modify synthetic content, audio, images, video or deepfakes?
help: Applies to generated or manipulated content that a person could interpret as real.

title: Do you train, fine-tune or commercialise your own foundation/GPAI models for critical sectors?
help: If you only consume a third-party API, you are usually a deployer; if you develop the model, review GPAI obligations.

title: Do you deploy a system developed by a third party?
help: For example AI SaaS, model APIs, copilots, HR tech, external scoring or modules integrated into your ERP/CRM.

title: Do you develop, sell or substantially modify the system before using it?
help: Changing purpose, logic, data or behaviour can effectively turn you into a provider.

title: Does it process personal data, special categories or children's data?
help: This does not change the AI Act classification by itself, but increases diligence around data, security and evidence.
